GET /v1/ticket/123 HTTP/1.1
Authorization: Basic <Base64 key>

schemas.title: Adaptive Links
schemas.description:
Adaptive links detect the user's platform and install the correct
pass for their device vendor. An adaptive link can specify an Apple and Google
template.

schemas.name: adaptiveLinkRequest
schemas.title: Adaptive Link request
schemas.description:
An adaptive link request contains identifiers for the templates you used to generate passes from the link and any fields you want to set when users create passes from the link.
If you need to create a boarding pass adaptive link, go to the [boarding pass object](/developer/rest-api/wallet/schemas/flights-and-boarding-passes/).

properties.name: androidTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Google template for passes issued from this
adaptive link.

properties.name: androidTemplateId
properties.description: The Google template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: availablePasses
properties.description: The total number of passes that can be created from this link. If absent, the link supports 1,000,000 passes.

properties.name: iosTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Apple template for passes issued from the
adaptive link, if assigned.

properties.name: iosTemplateId
properties.description: The iOS template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: isPersonalized
properties.description:
If true, each request (when a recipient uses an adaptive link) generates
a new pass. You can update any of the individual passes generated from
the adaptive link. If you generate the adaptive link using any request
parameters, `isPersonalized` is automatically set to `true`.


If false, only the first request generates a pass; subsequent requests
generate a new instance of the same pass. When `isPersonalized` is `false`,
you cannot update passes generated from an adaptive link by updating the
adaptive link itself. Rather, you must use the Bulk Update Passes endpoint
to push updates to the pass instances generated from the adaptive link.

properties.name: landingPageUrl
properties.description: The address users are redirected to if their device type cannot be detected or they cannot install the pass for another reason.

properties.name: parameterEncoding
properties.description: When set, allows url-encoded parameters to set or modify values when creating passes from the adaptive link.

properties.name: androidPassLinkId
properties.description: A dynamic link for Android passes.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description: When set, indicates the date this adaptive link expires. An expired adaptive link will return a JSON object with an error message instead of a pass.

properties.name: installLimit
properties.description:
The number of times a user can install the pass. When the pass reaches its limit, attempts to install the pass are met with errors.


This limit only affects the adaptive link itself, not actual Apple pass files. If you want to prevent users from installing shared passes on Apple devices, you should also set `sharingStatus` to prohibit sharing.

properties.name: installLimitPerPassExternalId
properties.description:
The number of times a user can install the pass that has an `externalId`. When the pass reaches its limit, attempts to install the pass are met with errors.


This limit only affects the adaptive link itself, not actual Apple pass files. If you want to prevent users from installing shared passes on Apple devices, you should also set `sharingStatus` to prohibit sharing.

properties.name: iosPassLinkId
properties.description: A dynamic link for iOS passes.

properties.name: locationRadius
properties.description:
Determines the range, in miles, that a pass holder can be from a location
to associate their pass with a location. If absent, the default location radius is 10 miles.

properties.name: locations

items.title: Location object
items.description:
Represents a location on a pass or an adaptive link.


Place objects in the locations array to add location information to passes and templates. Updating locations on a pass or template will replace all locations on that pass; if you want to add to the locations on a pass, you must provide all locations already included on the pass and any additional locations you want to add.

Apple Wallet Each pass supports up to 10 locations. If you exceed this limit for an iOS pass, we will use the 10 locations nearest to a user (located by IP address) when they install the pass.

properties.description:
An array of up to 10,000 locations associated with the adaptive link. Each pass supports up to 10 locations. If you exceed this limit, the passes will use the 10 locations nearest to a user (located by IP address) when they install the pass.


You can also include a location radius and the maximum number of locations to be matched upon pass creation. Wallet sorts locations to be matched in order from closest to/furthest from the location provided by the device.

properties.name: maxResultLocations
properties.description:
The maximum number of locations the pass recipient can match. If the pass holder
matches multiple locations, locations are returned in order from closest
to farthest.

properties.name: payload
properties.description:
The field names and values you want to update for those pass fields. Changing a value will result in a change message notifying the user of the changed value.


While the payload object is required, it can be an empty object.

properties.name: sharingStatus
properties.description:
A `field` determining whether passes can be shared across users, devices, or not at all. By default, there are no restrictions with regard to users or devices (`multipleHolders`). While this setting uses the same format as other `fields`, you only need to set the `value` within the object. Most other keys are irrelevant this setting, even though they appear in responses; this field should not be visible on passes, so you should not populate label, order, etc.

On iOS devices, `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing (`"sharingProhibited": true`); all other values allow sharing.

You can override the template setting on Apple Wallet passes. If you set this field in an adaptive link payload, it will only apply to Apple Wallet passes resulting from the adaptive link; Google Wallet passes will always use the sharing setting set at the template level.

properties.name: changeMessage
properties.description: The message that appears when you update this field.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Determines whether a pass supports sharing across users, devices, or both. iOS interprets this as a boolean setting: `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing; all other values allow sharing.

json:
{
  "iosTemplateId": 12345,
  "androidTemplateId": 154321,
  "isPersonalized": "true",
  "iosPassLinkId": "44e128a5-ac7a-4c9a-be4c-224b6bf81b20",
  "androidPassLinkId": "44e128a5-ac7a-4c9a-be4c-224b6bf81b20",
  "landingPageUrl": "https://example.com/landing.html",
  "expirationDate": "2022-10-01",
  "availablePasses": 100000,
  "ttlInDays": 30,
  "parameterEncoding": "base64",
  "locationRadius": 10,
  "maxResultLocations": 5,
  "payload": {},
  "locations": [
      {
         "latitude": 45.5898,
         "longitude": -122.5951,
         "city": "Portland",
         "country": "US",
         "region": "OR",
         "regionCode": "97218",
         "relevantText": "Welcome to Portland... Voodoo Donuts is only 11 miles away",
         "streetAddress1": "7000 NE Airport Way"
      },
      {
         "latitude": 45.525492,
         "longitude": -122.686092,
         "city": "San Francisco",
         "country": "US",
         "region": "CA",
         "regionCode": "94104",
         "relevantText": "Welcome to the Ship!",
         "streetAddress1": "548 Market St. Suite 698370",
         "streetAddress2": ""
      },
      {
         "latitude": 45.5205,
         "longitude": -122.6788,
         "relevantText": "See you at dinner tonight...or did you already fill up on donuts?"
      }
    ]
  }

schemas.name: adaptiveLinkResponse
schemas.title: Adaptive Link response
schemas.description: An adaptive link response includes URLs that users can access to detect and install a pass.

properties.name: androidTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Google template for passes issued from this
adaptive link.

properties.name: androidTemplateId
properties.description: The Google template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: availablePasses
properties.description: The total number of passes that can be created from this link. If absent, the link supports 1,000,000 passes.

properties.name: iosTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Apple template for passes issued from the
adaptive link, if assigned.

properties.name: iosTemplateId
properties.description: The iOS template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: isPersonalized
properties.description:
If true, each request (when a recipient uses an adaptive link) generates
a new pass. You can update any of the individual passes generated from
the adaptive link. If you generate the adaptive link using any request
parameters, `isPersonalized` is automatically set to `true`.


If false, only the first request generates a pass; subsequent requests
generate a new instance of the same pass. When `isPersonalized` is `false`,
you cannot update passes generated from an adaptive link by updating the
adaptive link itself. Rather, you must use the Bulk Update Passes endpoint
to push updates to the pass instances generated from the adaptive link.

properties.name: landingPageUrl
properties.description: The address users are redirected to if their device type cannot be detected or they cannot install the pass for another reason.

properties.name: parameterEncoding
properties.description: When set, allows url-encoded parameters to set or modify values when creating passes from the adaptive link.

properties.name: adaptiveLinkId
properties.description: A unique identifier for the adaptive link. Use this value to reference the adaptive link in future operations.

properties.name: androidPassLinkId
properties.description: A dynamic link for Android passes. This field cannot be provided for `links/adaptive/multiple` requests, nor is it provided in those responses (e.g., boarding passes, event tickets, etc.).

properties.name: androidUrl
properties.description: A pass URL specific to Android users.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description: The date this adaptive link expires. This property appears in the response only if it has been explicitly set with a previous API request. Cannot be set to a date more than 1,080 days in the future.

properties.name: iosPassLinkId
properties.description: A dynamic link for iOS passes. This field cannot be provided for `links/adaptive/multiple` requests, nor is it provided in those responses (e.g., boarding passes, event tickets, etc.).

properties.name: iosUrl
properties.description: A pass URL specific to iOS users.

properties.name: isExpired
properties.description: If true, the adaptive link is expired. An expired adaptive link will return a JSON object with an error message instead of a pass.

properties.name: projectId
properties.description: The ID of the project.

properties.name: ttlInDays
properties.description: The number of days of inactivity before this adaptive link automatically expires.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The adaptive link URL itself; using this URL on an Android or iOS device will detect device types and install the correct pass.

schemas.title: Event tickets
schemas.description: Schemas for creating events and event ticket adaptive links. An event ticket includes both event information and an array of attendees.

schemas.name: attendees
schemas.title: Attendees
schemas.description: An array of attendees for an event. Each object in the array represents a single attendee.

schemas.name: eventRequest
schemas.title: Event request
schemas.description: Represents an event scheduled at a specific time and venue.

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Contains the objects representing an event.

properties.name: doorsOpen
properties.description: The date and time when ticket holders can begin to enter the venue.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The title for this event field as you want it to appear on a pass. Defaults to `Doors Open`.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The date and time when the event ends.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The title for this event field, as you want it to appear on a pass. Defaults to `End Time`.

properties.name: eventName
properties.description: The value represents the event name.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for this field, as you want it to appear on the pass. If you do not provide a label, the event will use a default based off the parent object name.

properties.name: startTime
properties.description: The date and time when the event begins.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The title for this event field, as you want it to appear on a pass. Defaults to `Start Time`.

properties.name: venueAddress
properties.description: The address of the venue.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for this field, as you want it to appear on the pass. If you do not provide a label, the event will use a default based off the parent object name.

properties.name: venueTitle
properties.description: The name of the venue where the event will take place.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The label for this field, as you want it to appear on the pass. If you do not provide a label, the event will use a default based off the parent object name.

properties.name: passGroups
properties.description:
An array of eventId or eventExternalId values representing a group. You can reference the group to make changes to all associated events.

You can set pass groups when creating an event or when creating an event ticket adaptive link.

schemas.name: eventResponse
schemas.title: Event response
schemas.description: An event response returns identifiers that you can use to reference the event in other endpoints, along with the complete event request body.

compositeOptions.title: Event request
compositeOptions.description: Represents an event scheduled at a specific time and venue.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: eventExternalId
properties.description: An external identifier for an event. You can only assign an external identifier when creating an event. If you do assign an external identifier, requests for events or passes referencing the event will return the external ID in addition to the `eventId` created within Airship.

properties.name: eventId
properties.description: The Airship-created identifier for an event.

properties.name: projectExternalId
properties.description: Returned if you created the event using an external ID for the project.

properties.name: projectId
properties.description: The ID of the Wallet project.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

schemas.name: eventTicketRequest
schemas.title: Event ticket Adaptive Link request
schemas.description:
An event ticket requires similar information to other adaptive
link types, but does not support some of the same fields, and requires event and attendee information.


Like other adaptive links, you must provide the `id` or `externalId` of an iOS or Android template. You can create the event within this request or specify an event by `eventId` or `eventExternalId`. In either case, you must also provide an array of attendees for the event.

properties.description:

adaptive link.

adaptive link, if assigned.

properties.name: isPersonalized
properties.description:
If true, each request (when a recipient uses an adaptive link) generates
a new pass. You can update any of the individual passes generated from
the adaptive link. If you generate the adaptive link using any request
parameters, `isPersonalized` is automatically set to `true`.


If false, only the first request generates a pass; subsequent requests
generate a new instance of the same pass. When `isPersonalized` is `false`,
you cannot update passes generated from an adaptive link by updating the
adaptive link itself. Rather, you must use the Bulk Update Passes endpoint
to push updates to the pass instances generated from the adaptive link.

options.title: Existing Event by ID
options.description: If you specify the `eventId` or `eventExternalId` of an existing event, you need only provide the attendees for the event ticket.

properties.name: eventId
properties.description: The Airship-created identifier for an event.

properties.name: eventExternalId
properties.description: An external identifier for an event. You can only assign an external identifier when creating an event. If you do assign an external identifier, requests for events or passes referencing the event will return the external ID in addition to the `eventId` created within Airship.

properties.name: attendees
properties.schemaRefDescription: An array of attendees for an event. Each object in the array represents a single attendee.

properties.name: passGroups
properties.description:
An array of eventId or eventExternalId values representing a group. You can reference the group to make changes to all associated events.

You can set pass groups when creating an event or when creating an event ticket adaptive link.

options.title: Create New Event
options.description: If creating an event, you can provide an external ID for the event. You must then provide a complete event object.

properties.name: eventExternalId
properties.description: An external identifier for an event. You can only assign an external identifier when creating an event. If you do assign an external identifier, requests for events or passes referencing the event will return the external ID in addition to the `eventId` created within Airship.

options.title: Event request
options.description: Represents an event scheduled at a specific time and venue.

properties.name: attendees
properties.schemaRefDescription: An array of attendees for an event. Each object in the array represents a single attendee.

properties.description:
Each object in the array represents an event. Each event object must specify the `eventId` or `eventExternalId` of an existing event, or contain the `fields` object sufficient to create a new event.

You can also provide the `eventExternalId` in conjunction with the `fields` object to assign an eventExternalId to a new event.

schemas.title: Flights and boarding passes
schemas.description: Schemas for creating flights and boarding pass adaptive links. A Boarding pass includes both flight information and an array of passengers.

schemas.name: boardingPassRequest
schemas.title: Boarding pass Adaptive Link request
schemas.description:
A boarding pass adaptive link requires similar information to other adaptive
link types, but the payload includes flight information and an array of passengers for each flight.


Like other adaptive links, you must provide the `id` or `externalId` of an
iOS or Android template.

properties.name: androidTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Google template for passes issued from this
adaptive link.

properties.name: androidTemplateId
properties.description: The Google template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: availablePasses
properties.description: The total number of passes that can be created from this link. If absent, the link supports 1,000,000 passes.

properties.name: iosTemplateExternalId
properties.description:
The custom identifier of the Apple template for passes issued from the
adaptive link, if assigned.

properties.name: iosTemplateId
properties.description: The iOS template for passes issued from the adaptive link.

properties.name: isPersonalized
properties.description:
If true, each request (when a recipient uses an adaptive link) generates
a new pass. You can update any of the individual passes generated from
the adaptive link. If you generate the adaptive link using any request
parameters, `isPersonalized` is automatically set to `true`.


If false, only the first request generates a pass; subsequent requests
generate a new instance of the same pass. When `isPersonalized` is `false`,
you cannot update passes generated from an adaptive link by updating the
adaptive link itself. Rather, you must use the Bulk Update Passes endpoint
to push updates to the pass instances generated from the adaptive link.

properties.name: landingPageUrl
properties.description: The address users are redirected to if their device type cannot be detected or they cannot install the pass for another reason.

properties.name: parameterEncoding
properties.description: When set, allows url-encoded parameters to set or modify values when creating passes from the adaptive link.

properties.name: payload
properties.description:
A boarding pass adaptive link object takes both flight and passenger information.


You can provide the `flightId` or `flightExternalId` of an existing flight. Or, you can define a new flight using the `flights` object. If defining a new flight, you can provide a `flightExternalId`.

properties.name: flights

options.title: Flight by ID
options.description: You can specify the `flightId` or `flightExternalId` of an existing flight, and then you need only populate the passengers for the flight.

properties.name: flightId
properties.description:
A unique, auto-generated identifier for a flight.


When creating boarding passes,
if you specify a flight by ID, you do not need to define the flight in the
`fields` object.

properties.name: flightExternalId
properties.description:
An external, custom identifier for a flight. You can reference flights by
`flightExternalId` rather than the Airship-generated `flightId`.


When creating boarding passes, if you specify an existing flight by `flightExternalId`,
you do not need to provide flight information in the `fields` object. If creating
a new flight in the `fields` object, you can assign a new `flightExternalId`
to the new flight.

properties.name: passGroups
properties.description:
An array of eventId or eventExternalId values representing a group. You can reference the group to make changes to all associated events.

You can set pass groups when creating an event or when creating an event ticket adaptive link.

properties.name: passengers
properties.schemaRefDescription:
An array of objects, each object representing a passenger/seat on a flight.
Passengers are a part of the payload for boarding pass adaptive links.

options.title: Create a New Flight
options.description: You can create a flight as a part of a boarding pass request. You can provide a `flightExternalId` for the flight you are creating, and then provide a complete flight object in addition to the array of `passengers` for the flight.

properties.name: flightExternalId
properties.description:
An external, custom identifier for a flight. You can reference flights by
`flightExternalId` rather than the Airship-generated `flightId`.


When creating boarding passes, if you specify an existing flight by `flightExternalId`,
you do not need to provide flight information in the `fields` object. If creating
a new flight in the `fields` object, you can assign a new `flightExternalId`
to the new flight.

options.title: Flight object
options.description:
A complete flight request object.


The presence or absence of fields in the flight object may slightly affect the design of boarding passes. See [Google Wallet Boarding Pass Design](https://developers.google.com/pay/passes/guides/pass-verticals/boarding-passes/design) for more information on rendering logic for Google Wallet Boarding Passes.

properties.name: passengers
properties.schemaRefDescription:
An array of objects, each object representing a passenger/seat on a flight.
Passengers are a part of the payload for boarding pass adaptive links.

properties.description:
Each object in the array represents an event. Each event object must specify the `flightId` or `flightExternalId` of an existing event, or contain the `fields` object sufficient to create a new event.


You can also provide the `eventExternalId` in conjunction with the `fields` object to assign an eventExternalId to a new event.

properties.name: isEventTicketUpdatePermitted
properties.description: True by default. If false, event information for existing events will not be updated as part of the POST call to the Adaptive Link API; a new event will still be created if it does not yet exist.

properties.name: isFlightUpdatePermitted
properties.description: True by default. If false, flight information for existing flights will not be updated as part of the POST call to the Adaptive Link API; a new flight will still be created if it does not yet exist.

schemas.name: boardingPassResponse
schemas.title: Boarding pass Adaptive Link response
schemas.description:
The boarding pass operations return responses like other adaptive links, with the addition of the identifier of the event and an HTTP status for each individual adaptive link.


Like other adaptive links, you must provide the `id` or `externalId` of an
iOS or Android template.

compositeOptions.title: Adaptive Link response
compositeOptions.description: An adaptive link response includes URLs that users can access to detect and install a pass.

properties.name: flightExternalId
properties.description:
An external, custom identifier for a flight. You can reference flights by
`flightExternalId` rather than the Airship-generated `flightId`.


When creating boarding passes, if you specify an existing flight by `flightExternalId`,
you do not need to provide flight information in the `fields` object. If creating
a new flight in the `fields` object, you can assign a new `flightExternalId`
to the new flight.

properties.name: flightId
properties.description:
A unique, auto-generated identifier for a flight.


When creating boarding passes,
if you specify a flight by ID, you do not need to define the flight in the
`fields` object.

schemas.name: boardingPassSemantics
schemas.title: Boarding pass semantics
schemas.description: For Apple boarding passes only, a JSON object that uses key-value pairs to add rich, machine-readable data that enhances passes. You can nest this object within `fields`.

compositeOptions.title: Passenger semantics object
compositeOptions.description: For Apple boarding passes only, a JSON object that uses key-value pairs to add rich, machine-readable data, such as `passengerName` and `seats`, that enhances passes. You can nest this object within `fields`.

compositeOptions.title: Flight semantics object
compositeOptions.description: For Apple boarding passes only, a JSON object that uses key-value pairs to add rich, machine-readable data, such as `flightNumber` and `originalDepartureTime`, that enhances passes. You can nest this object within `fields`.

See [Google Wallet Boarding Pass Design](https://developers.google.com/pay/passes/guides/pass-verticals/boarding-passes/design) for more information on rendering logic for Google Wallet Boarding Passes.

schemas.title: Passes

schemas.name: createPassAppleWalletRequest
schemas.title: Apple Wallet pass request
schemas.description: A pass for Apple Wallet.

properties.name: beacons

items.title: Beacon object
items.description: Associates a pass with an iBeacon. Apple Wallet supports up to 10 beacons per pass. When the pass holder comes into proximity with an iBeacon bearing the same UUID, the pass becomes relevant (displayed on the lock screen).

properties.description: Beacons for an Apple pass.

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Fields for an Apple Wallet pass. Provide only the field objects and values you want to differentiate or personalize from the template.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Headers on an Apple Wallet pass. Provide only the header objects and values you want to differentiate or personalize from the template. See [Apple Template Headers](/developer/rest-api/wallet/schemas/others/#iostemplateheaderobject) for information about available header objects for Apple Wallet templates and passes.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description:
May contain `value` and `label` fields. `value` indicates the expiration date of the pass.
`label` can be set to either `valid` or `voided`, where `valid` indicates a non-expired pass and `voided` indicates that the pass is no longer accepted.

properties.name: sharingStatus
properties.description:
A `field` determining whether passes can be shared across users, devices, or not at all. By default, there are no restrictions with regard to users or devices (`multipleHolders`). While this setting uses the same format as other `fields`, you only need to set the `value` within the object. Most other keys are irrelevant this setting, even though they appear in responses; this field should not be visible on passes, so you should not populate label, order, etc.

On iOS devices, `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing (`"sharingProhibited": true`); all other values allow sharing.

You can override the template setting on Apple Wallet passes. If you set this field in an adaptive link payload, it will only apply to Apple Wallet passes resulting from the adaptive link; Google Wallet passes will always use the sharing setting set at the template level.

properties.name: changeMessage
properties.description: The message that appears when you update this field.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Determines whether a pass supports sharing across users, devices, or both. iOS interprets this as a boolean setting: `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing; all other values allow sharing.

properties.name: locations

items.title: Location object
items.description:
Represents a location on a pass or an adaptive link.


Place objects in the locations array to add location information to passes and templates. Updating locations on a pass or template will replace all locations on that pass; if you want to add to the locations on a pass, you must provide all locations already included on the pass and any additional locations you want to add.

Apple Wallet Each pass supports up to 10 locations. If you exceed this limit for an iOS pass, we will use the 10 locations nearest to a user (located by IP address) when they install the pass.

properties.description: A list of locations associated with a pass.

properties.name: nfc
properties.description: If your Apple certificate is NFC-enabled, and your terminals use Apple's Value Added Services protocol, you can include this object in Loyalty passes to identify pass holders and provide their credentials over NFC.

properties.name: encryptionPublicKey
properties.description: The public encryption key for NFC communications. Use a Base64 encoded X.509 `SubjectPublicKeyInfo` structure containing a ECDH public key for group P256.

properties.name: message
properties.description: The payload for an Apple Pay terminal, 64 bytes or less. Messages longer than 64 bytes are truncated.

properties.name: publicUrl
properties.description:
A public URL from which users can download a pass.

For Google Wallet, the public URL deep-links to the Google Wallet store with credentials to download a pass, facilitating pass installation without a browser window. When creating Google Wallet passes, this field is required and you must specify `type: single` in the request to create a pass. If the request does not contain a Public URL, the operation will not return a URL and users will not be able to access the resulting pass.

For Apple Wallet, this URL points to a .pkpass file. We recommend using a Public URL when creating Apple Wallet passes. If you do not provide a public URL, users will not be able to install the pass without the API key.

properties.name: installs
properties.description: The number of users who have installed the pass from the URL specified in the `path` field.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The URL for the pass.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Determines whether a user can access the link to install a pass one time
or multiple times. The response indicates the number of times the publicUrl
has been used to install a pass.

properties.name: used
properties.description: If true, a user has accessed the `path` URL.

properties.name: semantics
properties.description: Apple boarding pass only, top-level semantics.
properties.schemaRefDescription: For Apple boarding passes only, a JSON object that uses key-value pairs to add rich, machine-readable data that enhances passes. You can nest this object within `fields`.

properties.name: tags
properties.description: An array of tags associated with the pass.

properties.name: templateId
properties.description: The identifier for the template. You can recall the template by ID in other operations.

schemas.name: createPassAndroidPayRequest
schemas.title: Google Wallet pass request
schemas.description:
A pass for Google Wallet. Unlike templates, in which the `fieldsModel` contains fields nested inside `module` objects, fields are collapsed in pass requests and responses. The `fieldType` corresponds to the template field module that the field belongs to.

Aside from differences in field composition, and a lack of beacons, Google Wallet passes are very similar to Apple Wallet passes.

properties.name: fields
properties.description: Fields for the pass. The template defines a field's location on the pass and its default value(s). When creating a pass, you only need to populate the fields you want to update, and even then, you only need to provide the `value` you want to change for the field and the `changeMessage` if you want to notify pass holders of the change in the value.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Include objects from the template `headers` object if you want to change the `label`, `value`, or `changeMessage` for fields in the pass header — like barcodes and titles.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description:
May contain `value` and `label` fields. `value` indicates the expiration date of the pass.
`label` can be set to either `valid` or `voided`, where `valid` indicates a non-expired pass and `voided` indicates that the pass is no longer accepted.

properties.name: locations

items.title: Location object
items.description:
Represents a location on a pass or an adaptive link.


Place objects in the locations array to add location information to passes and templates. Updating locations on a pass or template will replace all locations on that pass; if you want to add to the locations on a pass, you must provide all locations already included on the pass and any additional locations you want to add.

Apple Wallet Each pass supports up to 10 locations. If you exceed this limit for an iOS pass, we will use the 10 locations nearest to a user (located by IP address) when they install the pass.

properties.description: A list of locations associated with a pass.

properties.name: publicUrl
properties.description:
A public URL from which users can download a pass.

For Google Wallet, the public URL deep-links to the Google Wallet store with credentials to download a pass, facilitating pass installation without a browser window. When creating Google Wallet passes, this field is required and you must specify `type: single` in the request to create a pass. If the request does not contain a Public URL, the operation will not return a URL and users will not be able to access the resulting pass.

For Apple Wallet, this URL points to a .pkpass file. We recommend using a Public URL when creating Apple Wallet passes. If you do not provide a public URL, users will not be able to install the pass without the API key.

properties.name: installs
properties.description: The number of users who have installed the pass from the URL specified in the `path` field.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The URL for the pass.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Determines whether a user can access the link to install a pass one time
or multiple times. The response indicates the number of times the publicUrl
has been used to install a pass.

properties.name: used
properties.description: If true, a user has accessed the `path` URL.

properties.name: templateId
properties.description: The identifier for the template. You can recall the template by ID in other operations.

schemas.name: createPassAndroidPayResponse
schemas.title: Google Wallet pass response
schemas.description:
A pass response for Google Wallet.  A pass is a populated template. Therefore, the pass includes all headers and fields from the template, along with identifiers for the pass and URLs to access it.

Unlike templates, in which the `fieldsModel` contains fields nested inside `module` objects, fields are collapsed in pass requests and responses. The `fieldType` corresponds to the template field module (an object) that the field belongs to.


Aside from differences in field composition, and a lack of beacons, Google Wallet passes are very similar to Apple Wallet passes.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: externalId
properties.description: The external ID for the pass, returned only if you created the pass using an external ID.

properties.name: fields
properties.description:
Contains all populated fields from the template. The `fieldType` within each object indicates the `module` it belonged to.


While all fields take a similar shape, the response may contain different keys to represent the `value` or `label` from the originating template field. This has to do with the way that Google Wallet interprets templates and passes.

properties.name: offerModule
properties.description: Specific to google coupons, this module contains information about redeeming the coupon.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The expiration date for the offer.

properties.name: multiUseOffer
properties.description: Indicates whether the coupon/offer is available for multiple users or just a single user.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: The offer provider name.

properties.name: redemptionChannel
properties.description: Indicates whether the user can redeem the offer at a physical location or online.

properties.name: headers
properties.schemaRefDescription: Fields appearing in the `headers` object for a Google pass template. Header fields typically follow the same model as other fields for Google Wallet templates and passes, but often have specific `value`, `fieldType`, and `formatType` values.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The internal identifier for the pass. Use this ID to get or modify the pass in other calls.

properties.name: publicUrl
properties.description:
A public URL from which users can download a pass.

For Google Wallet, the public URL deep-links to the Google Wallet store with credentials to download a pass, facilitating pass installation without a browser window. When creating Google Wallet passes, this field is required and you must specify `type: single` in the request to create a pass. If the request does not contain a Public URL, the operation will not return a URL and users will not be able to access the resulting pass.

For Apple Wallet, this URL points to a .pkpass file. We recommend using a Public URL when creating Apple Wallet passes. If you do not provide a public URL, users will not be able to install the pass without the API key.

properties.name: installs
properties.description: The number of users who have installed the pass from the URL specified in the `path` field.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The URL for the pass.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Determines whether a user can access the link to install a pass one time
or multiple times. The response indicates the number of times the publicUrl
has been used to install a pass.

properties.name: used
properties.description: If true, a user has accessed the `path` URL.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Indicates whether a pass has been installed or not.

properties.name: tags
properties.description: Tags associated with the pass.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL for the pass.

schemas.title: Project objects
schemas.description: Request and Response schemas for `/project` endpoints.

schemas.name: projectRequest
schemas.title: Project request
schemas.description: A project request determines the type of passes you can create and the types of barcode your passes will use.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description for the project.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of your project.

properties.name: projectType
properties.description: The type of pass the template supports; matches the `type` setting for the parent project.

properties.name: settings
properties.description: Contains barcode information for the project.

properties.name: barcode_alt_text
properties.description: Alternate text for the barcode. This text assists the user if they hover over the barcode or the barcode doesn't render.

properties.name: barcode_default_value
properties.description: The default value for the barcode. If you do not set a new value when creating a pass, the pass will use this value.

properties.name: barcode_encoding
properties.description:
Barcode encoding is set at the project level and inherited by templates
and passes.

properties.name: barcode_label
properties.description: The title of the barcode; appears above the barcode in templates. You can change this value when creating or updating templates or passes.

properties.name: barcode_type
properties.description: The format of the barcode supported by the project and resulting passes.

schemas.name: projectPayload
schemas.title: Project response
schemas.description: A project response includes all fields in a project request, along with identifiers for the project and a list of templates created within the project.

compositeOptions.title: Project request
compositeOptions.description: A project request determines the type of passes you can create and the types of barcode your passes will use.

properties.name: contextId
properties.description: Append this value to `go.urbanairship.com/projects/` to access your project.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: externalId
properties.description: The custom, external identifier of the project. This key only appears if you assigned an external ID to the project.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The identifier for the project, used to reference the project in other payloads.

properties.name: templates
createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The identifier for the template. You can recall the template by ID in other operations.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

options.title: General template headers
options.description: Meta information about templates; this object appears on all templates and identifies templates associated with a project.

properties.description: An array of templates belonging to the project. When creating a new project, this array is empty.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

schemas.title: Template objects
schemas.description: Template objects vary based on the vendor they're intended for and the types of passes you want to create.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the rewards program that the recipient is signing up for. The description appears on the signup sheet, under the personalization logo.

properties.name: imageUrl
properties.description: The URL of the image you want to appear at the top of the signup form. This image must be a 150 x 40 point PNG image.

properties.name: requiredPersonalizationFields
properties.description:
An array of strings representing fields that a customer must provide to sign up for your rewards/loyalty program. Some keys populate multiple fields in the personalization callback or on passes.

* name - requires the user to enter their `fullName`. This also populates the `givenName` and `familyName` fields on passes and/or personalization callbacks.
* postalCode - prompts the user for their postal code. This populates both `postalCode` and `ISOCountryCode` on passes and/or personalization callbacks.
* emailAddress - requires the user's email address.
* phoneNumber - requires the user's phone number.

properties.name: termsAndConditions
properties.description: The terms and conditions for the reward program. If present, this information appears after the user enters their personal information and taps Next. The user then has the option to agree to the terms, or to cancel the signup process.

schemas.name: iosTemplate
schemas.title: Apple Wallet template request
schemas.description: A complete iOS template includes template meta information, headers, and fields.

compositeOptions.title: General template headers
compositeOptions.description: Meta information about templates; this object appears on all templates and identifies templates associated with a project.

compositeOptions.title: iOS template headers
compositeOptions.description:
The iOS template `headers` object can contain the following objects. Each object
has a `formatType`, `fieldType`, and `value`.

* `formatType` is always `1`, indicating that the `value` is a string.

* `fieldType` is `topLevel` — a text or color value on the top-front of the pass, `image`, or `barcode`.

* `value` is the default value for the header field.

compositeOptions.title: iOS fields
compositeOptions.description: Defines fields on iOS templates and subsequent passes generated from the template.

schemas.name: googleTemplate
schemas.title: Google Wallet template request
schemas.description: A google template organizes fields into a series of module objects. Include only the objects you want to populate for a particular template; some modules may not apply to your template type.

compositeOptions.title: General template headers
compositeOptions.description: Meta information about templates; this object appears on all templates and identifies templates associated with a project.

compositeOptions.title: Google fields
compositeOptions.description: Fields on a Google pass are organized into modules. Modules define how fields on the pass are organized and appear. Fields within each module are objects with a string names.

schemas.name: googleHeaderItemShared
schemas.title: Optional fields for Google headers
schemas.description: Header objects for Google Wallet templates use many of the same keys as fields on the template/pass. Populate these keys as necessary. Template responses include these keys with their default values.

properties.name: changeMessage
properties.description: The message that appears when you update this field.

properties.name: hideEmpty
properties.description: If true, the field is hidden if empty.

properties.name: ignoresTimeZone
properties.description: if true, the date-time value in a field on an Apple Wallet pass is not offset to account for the pass recipient's time zone. This may be helpful for things like boarding passes or events, where your times are set local to an airport or venue and should not change based on a user's device. When applied to a non-date-time field or a Google template, this setting is ignored.

properties.name: label
properties.description: In most cases, you should not set a label for a Google template header. In responses, the label is typically an empty string.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Indicates whether or not the field is required on passes created from this template.

properties.name: textAlignment
properties.description: The alignment of text on the pass.

examples.description: Example Google header object with optional keys

schemas.title: Additional Schemas
schemas.description: Schemas that are not grouped by a specific tag. These include core data objects and shared schemas used across multiple operations.

schemas.name: iosTemplateHeaderObject
schemas.title: Apple template header object
schemas.description: An object in `headers` for an iOS template or pass contains the following items.

properties.name: fieldType
properties.description: The type of field for the iOS header.

properties.name: formatType
properties.description: Indicates that the `formatType` for the field is a string.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The default value for the field/header. While the value is always a string, it takes on a different format based on the `fieldType` and purpose.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date-time when the pass was created.

properties.name: externalId
properties.description: The external ID for the pass, if set.

properties.name: passId
properties.description: The ID of the pass installed or uninstalled.

properties.name: platform
properties.description: The platform on which the pass is installed.

properties.name: serialNumber
properties.description: The serial number of the pass. This string is generated by the vendor — Apple or Google.

properties.name: templateId
properties.description: The ID of the template the pass was created from.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date-time when the pass was last updated.

schemas.name: callbackPersonalizationObject
schemas.title: Callback personalization object
schemas.description: You must [add personalization requirements](/developer/rest-api/wallet/operations/apple-wallet-pass-personalization/) to Apple templates before users can personalize passes created from them.

properties.name: certificate
properties.description: A base64-encoded string of the p12 certificate with the private key.

properties.name: comment
properties.description: A description for the certificate.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was created.

properties.name: default
properties.description: If true, the certificate is the default for new projects. If you have multiple certificates and set a new default to `true`, the current default is set to `false`.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Indicates whether or not the certificate is in use.

properties.name: expired
properties.description: If true, the certificate has expired and cannot be used to generate new passes.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A name for the certificate.

properties.name: nfcSupport
properties.description: If true, the certificate supports passes that can make use of NFC.

properties.name: password
properties.description: The password for the p12 file, if the certificate was exported with a password.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: The date and time when the item was last updated.

properties.name: vendor
properties.description: The vendor of the certificate.

schemas.name: templateRequestHeaders
schemas.title: General template headers
schemas.description: Meta information about templates; this object appears on all templates and identifies templates associated with a project.

properties.name: deleted
properties.description:
If true, the template is deleted. You can no longer create passes from this
template.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description for the template.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description:
If true, the template is disabled; you cannot create new passes for this template
until you update the template and enable it again.

properties.name: expiryInfo
properties.description: Determine when passes generated from the template should expire.

compositeOptions.title: Expire on a date
compositeOptions.description: Set the specific expiration date for passes generated from this template. Passes expire at 12:00 AM on the date you provide.

properties.name: expiryDate
properties.description: The date when passes expire.

properties.name: expiryTimeZone
properties.description: Passes expire at 12:00 AM in the time zone you set.

compositeOptions.title: Expire after
compositeOptions.description: Expire passes generated from this template after the specified number of minutes after creation.

properties.name: expiryDuration
properties.description: The number of days after creation that passes will expire.

compositeOptions.title: Never expire
compositeOptions.description: Passes generated from the template will never expire.

properties.name: expireNever
properties.description: Any string value (or null) will prevent passes generated from this template from expiring.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the template.

properties.name: projectId
properties.description: The ID of the Wallet project.

properties.name: projectType
properties.description: The type of pass the template supports; matches the `type` setting for the parent project.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of pass the template supports. This value corresponds to the `projectType`.

properties.name: vendor
properties.description: The device vendor the template is designed for.

properties.name: vendorId
properties.description:
Corresponds to the `vendor` the template supports. `1` indicates an Apple
template; `2` indicates a Google template.

schemas.name: googleTemplateRequestFieldsModel
schemas.title: Google fields
schemas.description: Fields on a Google pass are organized into modules. Modules define how fields on the pass are organized and appear. Fields within each module are objects with a string names.

properties.name: acctModule
properties.description: Information associated with an account or membership. These are typically items like the `accountIdLabel` on Loyalty cards or `balance` items on a gift card.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: Google template headers define barcode and top-level information for passes.
properties.schemaRefDescription: Fields appearing in the `headers` object for a Google pass template. Header fields typically follow the same model as other fields for Google Wallet templates and passes, but often have specific `value`, `fieldType`, and `formatType` values.

properties.name: imageModulesData
properties.description: Contains an image on the pass outside the header.

properties.name: infoModuleData
properties.description: Info module

properties.name: linkModulesData
properties.description: Contains links including URLs, phone numbers, and email addresses, that you want to make available on the pass. This module typically appears at the bottom of the pass.

properties.name: offerModule
properties.description: Specific to google coupons, this module contains information about redeeming the coupon.

properties.name: endTime
properties.description: The expiration date for the offer.

properties.name: multiUseOffer
properties.description: Indicates whether the coupon/offer is available for multiple users or just a single user.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: The offer provider name.

properties.name: redemptionChannel
properties.description: Indicates whether the user can redeem the offer at a physical location or online.

properties.name: pointsModule
properties.description: Contains information related to `points` that pass users accrue or spend. This module is typically used on loyalty passes.

properties.name: textModulesData
properties.description: Contains text information for a pass. The text module typically has information in a header-body format.

properties.name: titleModule
properties.description: Contains title and header information specific to each pass type (as opposed to common items held within `headers`). These are items typically referenced by `class` in Google pass designs.

properties.name: sharingStatus
properties.description:
A `field` determining whether passes can be shared across users, devices, or not at all. By default, there are no restrictions with regard to users or devices (`multipleHolders`). While this setting uses the same format as other `fields`, you only need to set the `value` within the object. Most other keys are irrelevant this setting, even though they appear in responses; this field should not be visible on passes, so you should not populate label, order, etc.

On iOS devices, `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing (`"sharingProhibited": true`); all other values allow sharing.

You can override the template setting on Apple Wallet passes. If you set this field in an adaptive link payload, it will only apply to Apple Wallet passes resulting from the adaptive link; Google Wallet passes will always use the sharing setting set at the template level.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Determines whether a pass supports sharing across users, devices, or both. iOS interprets this as a boolean setting: `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing; all other values allow sharing.

properties.name: sharingStatus
properties.description:
A `field` determining whether passes can be shared across users, devices, or not at all. By default, there are no restrictions with regard to users or devices (`multipleHolders`). While this setting uses the same format as other `fields`, you only need to set the `value` within the object. Most other keys are irrelevant this setting, even though they appear in responses; this field should not be visible on passes, so you should not populate label, order, etc.

On iOS devices, `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing (`"sharingProhibited": true`); all other values allow sharing.

You can override the template setting on Apple Wallet passes. If you set this field in an adaptive link payload, it will only apply to Apple Wallet passes resulting from the adaptive link; Google Wallet passes will always use the sharing setting set at the template level.

properties.name: changeMessage
properties.description: The message that appears when you update this field.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Determines whether a pass supports sharing across users, devices, or both. iOS interprets this as a boolean setting: `oneUserOneDevice` prohibits sharing; all other values allow sharing.

properties.description: The image residing in the barcode strip.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The URL for the header image.

properties.name: suppress_strip_shine
properties.description: Determines whether or not to suppress the strip shine effect on barcodes.

schemas.name: locationObject
schemas.title: Location object
schemas.description:
Represents a location on a pass or an adaptive link.


Place objects in the locations array to add location information to passes and templates. Updating locations on a pass or template will replace all locations on that pass; if you want to add to the locations on a pass, you must provide all locations already included on the pass and any additional locations you want to add.

Apple Wallet Each pass supports up to 10 locations. If you exceed this limit for an iOS pass, we will use the 10 locations nearest to a user (located by IP address) when they install the pass.

properties.name: city
properties.description: The location city.

properties.name: country
properties.description: The country abbreviation or name.

properties.name: latitude
properties.description: The latitude of the location.

properties.name: longitude
properties.description: The longitude of the location.

properties.name: region
properties.description: The region or state name.

properties.name: regionCode
properties.description: The region or zip code.

properties.name: relevantText
properties.description: An optional value shown as lock screen text for iOS when the device is close to this location.

properties.name: streetAddress1
properties.description: The first line of the location address.

properties.name: streetAddress2
properties.description: The second line of the location address.

schemas.description: Contains information about pagination, according to your query parameters.

properties.name: direction
properties.description: The direction of results, ascending, or descending.

properties.name: order
properties.description: The key in the result set that you want to order results by. Defaults to `id`.

properties.name: page
properties.description: The page you are on. Multiply by the page size to determine the result set on the page.

properties.name: pageSize
properties.description: The number of results per page.

properties.name: start
properties.description: The first result on the page; results begin with 0.

schemas.name: fields
schemas.title: Pass field updates
schemas.description:
When updating a field on a pass or an adaptive link, you need only provide
the following items. You cannot update the position of the field or other
information held by the template — only the field label, value, and the message
the user receives when their pass is updated.

properties.name: changeMessage
properties.description: The message that appears when you change the value or label for a field. Use `%@` to pass variables to the field.

properties.name: label
properties.description: The field label, usually represented as a title on the pass. Only provide the label if you want to use a different label than is provided by the template.

properties.name: semantics
properties.description: Field-level semantics.
properties.schemaRefDescription: For Apple boarding passes only, a JSON object that uses key-value pairs to add rich, machine-readable data that enhances passes. You can nest this object within `fields`.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The default value for the field.

examples.description: Example update to a field called "Coupon"
